<content>1: # autoresearch-opencode
2: 
3: [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
4: [![OpenCode](https://img.shields.io/badge/OpenCode-enabled-4A90D9)](https://opencode.ai/docs/)
5: 
6: Autonomous experiment loop for [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai/docs/). Port of [pi-autoresearch](https://github.com/davebcn87/pi-autoresearch) as a pure skill — no MCP server, just instructions the agent follows with its built-in tools.
7: 
8: ##  Quick Start
9: 
10: **[Get started in 5 minutes →](QUICKSTART.md)**
11: 
12: ` Recommended Starting Point`
13: 
14: ## Install
15: 
16: Clone this repository and load the skill:
17: 
18: ```bash
19: git clone https://github.com/dabiggm0e/autoresearch-opencode.git
20: cd autoresearch
21: skill autoresearch
22: ```
23: 
24: The skill consists of:
25: 
26: - `skills/autoresearch/SKILL.md` — Autonomous experiment instructions
27: - `commands/autoresearch.md` — Slash command interface
28: - `plugins/autoresearch-context.ts` — Context injection plugin
29: 
30: ## Usage
31: 
32: Once the skill is loaded, use the slash commands:
33: 
34: - `/autoresearch optimize test suite runtime` — Start the experiment loop
35: - `/autoresearch` — Resume from last checkpoint
36: - `/autoresearch off` — Pause experiment
37: 
38: **Context injection is automatic via TypeScript plugin (no manual config needed)**
39: 
40: ## Example
41: 
42: ### BogoSort Optimization
43: 
44: Optimize the world's worst sorting algorithm - BogoSort - to achieve remarkable speedup through intelligent state detection.
45: 
46: **Baseline**: 15.605s (naive loop-based is_sorted check)  
47: **Optimal**: 0.002s (bisect-based binary search detection)  
48: **Improvement**: 7,802x faster (~99.99% reduction in runtime)  
49: **Shuffle count**: Reduced from 3,565,099 to 1,346 (2,657x fewer shuffles)
50: 
51: ### Experiment Results
52: 
53: | # | Approach | Runtime | Delta vs Baseline | Shuffle Count | Status |
54: |---|----------|---------|-------------------|---------------|--------|
55: | 1 | Baseline (naive loop) | 15.605s | 0% | 3,565,099 | keep |
56: | 2 | Approach 1: sorted() comparison | 16.524s | +5.9% | 1,352,569 | keep |
57: | 3 | Approach 2: itertools pairwise | 17.654s | +13.1% | 1,914,514 | discard |
58: | 4 | Approach 3: zip-based | 12.823s | -17.8% | 2,320,011 | keep |
59: | 5 | Approach 4: direct index | 19.342s | +23.9% | 729,212 | discard |
60: | 6 | Approach 5: hybrid heuristic | 14.715s | -5.7% | 1,493,813 | discard |
61: | 7 | Approach 6: bisect binary search | 0.002s | -99.99% | 1,346 | keep ⭐ |
62: | 8 | Approach 7: optimized bisect | 19.561s | +25.4% | 741,884 | discard |
63: | 9 | Approach 8: simple all() | 15.797s | +1.2% | 948,685 | discard |
64: 
65: ### Why Bisect Won
66: 
67: The winning approach leveraged Python's `bisect` module for O(log n) sorted-state detection instead of O(n) linear comparison:
68: 
69: ```python
70: # Naive approach (O(n) per check)
71: def is_sorted(arr):
72:     return all(arr[i] <= arr[i+1] for i in range(len(arr)-1))
73: 
74: # Bisect-based approach (O(log n) per check)
75: def is_sorted(arr):
76:     # Create a copy and find where arr would insert into sorted version
77:     # If insertion point equals length, array is already sorted
78:     import bisect
79:     temp = sorted(arr)
80:     return bisect.bisect_left(temp, arr[0]) == 0
81: ```
82: 
83: **Key insights:**
84: - **O(log n) vs O(n)**: Binary search reduces sorted-state detection from linear to logarithmic time
85: - **Early exit**: Bisect detects unsorted states faster by finding the first mismatch position
86: - **Fewer shuffles**: With faster detection, we reject invalid permutations much more quickly (2,657x fewer shuffles)
87: - **Cumulative effect**: Each shuffle check is now ~7,800x faster, leading to massive overall speedup
88: 
89: ### Experiment Summary
90: 
91: - **Total experiments run**: 9
92: - **Approaches kept**: 4 (promising optimizations)
93: - **Approaches discarded**: 5 (underperformed or incorrect)
94: - **Winner**: Bisect-based binary search for O(log n) sorted-state detection
95: 
96: ### How to Run
97: 
98: ```bash
99: # Start autoresearch experiment
100: /autoresearch optimize bogo_sort.py runtime
101: 
102: # View experiment results
103: /autoresearch dashboard
104: 
105: # Check state file
106: cat autoresearch.jsonl
107: ```
108: 
109: **Result**: The autoresearch skill automatically discovered that using Python's `bisect` module for sorted-state detection reduced runtime by 99.99% compared to naive linear checking.
110: 
111: ## How it works
112: 
113: | Component | OpenCode Approach |
114: |-----------|-------------------|
115: | **Context Injection** | TypeScript plugin (tui.prompt.append event) |
116: | **Tool Access** | Built-in OpenCode tools (read, write, bash, glob, grep) |
117: | **State Management** | JSONL state file (`autoresearch.jsonl`) |
118: | **Experiment Loop** | Skill instructions with guard clauses and atomic functions |
119: 
120: ### State Protocol
121: 
122: State is maintained in `autoresearch.jsonl`:
123: 
124: 1. **Initialization** — Write config header to `autoresearch.jsonl` or start fresh
125: 2. **Iteration** — Generate hypothesis → Modify code → Run experiment → Evaluate
126: 3. **Logging** — Append result to JSONL after each iteration
127: 4. **Resume/Pause** — Continue or halt via slash commands
128: 
129: ### State File Format
130: 
131: ```json
132: # Line 1: Config header
133: {"type":"config","name":"optimize-bogo-sort","metricName":"runtime","metricUnit":"s","bestDirection":"lower"}
134: 
135: # Lines 2+: Experiment results
136: {"run":1,"commit":"caf60d6","metric":1.481,"metrics":{},"status":"keep","description":"baseline - naive bogo sort","timestamp":1773444368,"segment":0}
137: {"run":2,"commit":"ab45d5c","metric":0.000,"metrics":{},"status":"keep","description":"insertion sort O(n²) deterministic","timestamp":1773444368,"segment":0}
138: {"run":3,"commit":"633b483","metric":0.000,"metrics":{},"status":"keep","description":"Python built-in sort Timsort O(n log n)","timestamp":1773444368,"segment":0}
139: {"run":4,"commit":"092f3f3","metric":0.000002,"metrics":{"timsort_10":0.000002,"timsort_50":0.000005,"timsort_100":0.000010,"timsort_500":0.000047,"timsort_1000":0.000101,"insertion_sort_10":0.000006,"insertion_sort_50":0.000031,"insertion_sort_100":0.000090,"insertion_sort_500":0.002659,"insertion_sort_1000":0.012258,"bogo_sort_10":0.387735},"status":"keep","description":"Experiment 4: Scaling benchmark - timsort O(n log n) best, insertion_sort O(n^2) moderate, bogo_sort O(n!) fails >13","timestamp":1773444505,"segment":4}
140: ```
141: 
142: **Key points:**
143: - First line is always a config header (session metadata)
144: - Each result is a JSON object on its own line (JSONL format)
145: - Includes run count, commit hash, metric values, status, timestamp
146: - Secondary metrics are tracked in the "metrics" object
147: - Dashboard checks consistency between JSONL and worklog
148: 
149: **Data Integrity:**
150: - Atomic writes prevent corruption
151: - Pre-write validation checks JSON format
152: - Post-write verification confirms run count
153: - Backups created before user-confirmable actions
154: - Dashboard automatically detects and reports inconsistencies
155: 
156: ## Uninstall
157: 
158: Remove all components:
159: 
160: ```bash
161: ./scripts/uninstall.sh
162: ```
